name: MCP Prompt Server
what_it_does: MCP Prompt Server providing a centralized prompt library and management system for AI agents — storing, versioning, and retrieving prompt templates via MCP's prompt primitive, enabling agents to access a shared repository of tested prompts for consistent, reusable instructions across agent workflows.
use_cases: Storing and retrieving reusable prompt templates from agent orchestration systems
Centralizing prompt management across multiple agents from prompt engineering teams
Version-controlling prompt iterations from experimentation agents
Sharing prompts across team members via centralized MCP service
Building prompt libraries for consistent agent behavior from standardization agents
A/B testing prompts with versioned prompt storage
not_for: Teams happy with inline prompts in code
Single-agent workflows that don't need shared prompts
Teams needing full MLOps prompt management (use Langfuse or Promptlayer)
best_when: A team manages many agents with shared prompt templates — centralizing prompt storage prevents duplication and enables prompt engineering at scale.
avoid_when: You have few agents or prompts — overhead of running a prompt server isn't worth it. Use Langfuse for full MLOps prompt management.

auth.notes: No authentication — local prompt storage. All agents connecting to the server can access all prompts.

pricing.notes: Free community MCP. No external service costs.

known_agent_gotchas: No authentication — any connected agent can read all stored prompts
Prompt storage is local — no cloud sync across machines
Community MCP — uses MCP prompt primitive which is less commonly used than tools
Prompt versioning capabilities vary by implementation
Client must support MCP prompt primitive (not all do)
Individual contributor project — limited long-term maintenance guarantee
agent_readiness.security_notes: No auth — all prompts accessible to any connected client. Local storage. Community MCP. Don't store sensitive secrets in prompts.

security_score_components.security_notes: No auth. Local storage. Don't store secrets in prompts. Community MCP.
